steps.title: Turn no real solutions into a boundary
steps.content: A quadratic has no real solutions when the discriminant is negative. For $x^2 - 36x + c = 0$, we have $a = 1$ and $b = -36$. The discriminant is $(-36)^2 - 4(1)(c) = 1296 - 4c$. For no real solutions: $1296 - 4c \lt 0$.

steps.title: Find the boundary where the discriminant becomes zero
steps.content: The equation transitions from one solution to zero solutions when $1296 - 4c = 0$. Solving: $4c = 1296$, so $c = 324$. Since no real solutions occur when $c \gt 324$, the least possible value of $n$ is 324.
